_meta.basis: USD billions. FY2025 actual company revenue ~$122.4B (TWD 3,809.1B). Paths are built as identities in sourced drivers (AI-accelerator CAGR x pool, node-ASP, segment growth), NOT a hand-drawn fade. Normalization year FY2029 (TSMC's guidance window), with a read to ~2030.

anchor_2025.note: 1Q26 mix is HPC 61% / Smartphone 26% as HPC accelerates; full-year 2025 HPC averaged ~55%. AI-accelerator slice ~17-19% of total (~$21-23B).

method: Whole-company is dominated by the HPC/AI growth segment, so the path is driven by the crux variable (AI-accelerator revenue CAGR x its weight) layered on a slower-growing base (non-AI HPC, smartphone, IoT/auto/other). Each path's implied AI-accelerator dollar size at FY2029 is checked against the sourced AI demand-pool ceiling so no path implies an impossible end-market.

segment_paths.driver_identity: HPC revenue = AI-accelerator revenue (pool x TSMC ASP, TSMC makes ~100% of merchant+ASIC) + non-AI HPC (CPU/networking, ~mid-teens % growth). AI-accelerator is the swing term.

base.note: AI-accelerator (~$22B 2025) at ~52% CAGR -> ~$120B by 2029; non-AI HPC (~$45B) at ~12% -> ~$70B; plus packaging revenue lift. Implied AI-accelerator pool at TSMC ~$120B sits well inside the >$500B-by-2028 data-center accelerator TAM (TSMC makes the silicon for effectively all of it). PASSES ceiling.

bull.note: AI-accelerator -> ~$145B+ by 2029 on a >$1T-by-2030 pool; still a minority of the sourced bull TAM. PASSES ceiling.

bear.note: Demand digestion after the 2024-27 build; AI-accelerator -> ~$72B by 2029. Comfortably inside any pool. PASSES ceiling.

segment_paths.nature: HIGH-GROWTH — the value driver. Value this segment exit-multiple.

segment_paths.driver_identity: Units ~flat-to-low-single-digit x rising leading-edge content per phone (Apple/flagship moving to N2 at higher ASP).
segment_paths.nature: MATURE-CYCLICAL with a content tailwind. Value current/near-term earnings x multiple.

segment_paths.driver_identity: Cyclical recovery off trough + secular content-per-device (edge AI, ADAS).

base.implied_cagr_2024_29: ~25% (aligns with TSMC's own raised 25% USD guide)
base.argument: Sum of segment paths -> ~$284B FY2029, a ~23-24% CAGR off 2025 (~25% off the 2024 base TSMC guides to). This is management's contracted+extrapolated frame taken at face value: the near term is booked (N2 sold out to 2Q27, FY26 >30%) and the AI slice compounds in the low-50s% on real capacity. The honest most-likely.

bull.argument: AI proves a durable megatrend (>$1T pool by 2030); TSMC prices harder and CoWoS adds a fat line. 25% guide proves conservative.

bear.argument: AI digestion + a 2027-28 leading-edge air-pocket + overseas dilution and TWD on margin. Share intact; the pool just grows slower than guided. A demand bear, not a share bear.

ceiling_check.method: Each path's implied AI-accelerator revenue at TSMC ÷ (TSMC makes ~100% of AI silicon) = the implied AI-accelerator end-pool the path requires. Compared to sourced TAM.
ceiling_check.sourced_ceiling_2028_2029: >$500B data-center AI accelerator TAM by 2028 (AMD), >$1T/yr by 2030 (bull houses).
ceiling_check.result: Base AI-accelerator at TSMC ~$120B (2029), bull ~$145B, bear ~$72B — all are a minority slice of the >$500B-$1T accelerator TAM (the TAM is end-product value; TSMC captures the wafer+packaging layer beneath it). NO path implies an impossible end-market. PASS.

handoff_note: Daedalus: split SOTP into (1) HPC/AI = exit-multiple on the FY2029 normalized earnings (the crux segment, own WACC reflecting concentration + cyclicality), (2) Smartphone = mature multiple on near-term earnings, (3) IoT/Auto = through-cycle, (4) DCE/Other = annuity. The base company path (~$284B FY2029 rev, ~25% CAGR 2024-29, ~57-59% GM) is the honest anchor; bull/bear above are the crux's higher/lower readings, not dialed cones. Net cash ~$54B (TWD ~1,703B) ADDS to equity value — do not subtract debt.

sources.value: AI accel CAGR 54-56% 2024-29; FY26 rev >30% USD; 1Q26 GM 66.2%
AI accelerator ~17-19% of 2025 revenue (~$21-23B); FY2025 rev $122.4B
Data-center AI accelerator TAM >$500B by 2028; >$1T/yr by 2030 (bull)
Platform mix 1Q26 HPC 61% / Smartphone 26% / IoT 6% / Auto 4%
